campaigns.name: Orchestration Authority
campaigns.objective: Position Danox as the leading thought leader on AI workflow orchestration, driving 20% LinkedIn growth and 15 inbound consulting inquiries per month within 90 days
campaigns.targetAudience: Founders, CTOs, COOs, and CMOs at AI/SaaS startups (pre-seed to Series B), mid-market companies ($10M–$100M) undergoing digital transformation, who face implementation challenges as their primary barrier to AI adoption
campaigns.positioning: AI workflow orchestration that connects end-to-end processes with human-in-the-loop governance — transforming fragmented tools into unified intelligence infrastructure rather than isolated automation tasks
keyMessages: Most AI initiatives fail at scale because they automate isolated tasks instead of orchestrating complete workflows
True competitive advantage comes from systems thinking — connecting data, decisions, and actions across your entire tech stack
AI should augment human judgment, not replace it — ethical governance and structured handover make automation sustainable
contentThemes: The Orchestration Gap — why PoCs fail and how to build production-ready systems from day one
System Thinking for AI — connecting data silos, automating decision chains, and creating unified workflows
Human-in-the-Loop Automation — designing AI systems that augment judgment with ethical governance and structured handover
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture through thought leadership posts, native carousels, and profile optimization
Phase 2: Consideration — nurture and educate with deep-dive posts, webinar series, and case study narratives
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests and SQL handoff through direct outreach, personalized content offers, and consultation calls
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture

campaigns.name: Ethical AI Governance
campaigns.objective: Build authority around ethical AI implementation, attracting 10% of total inbound leads from organizations concerned with compliance and governance as a competitive differentiator
campaigns.targetAudience: Enterprise digital transformation teams, compliance officers, and CTOs at mid-market companies ($10M–$100M) prioritizing ethical AI, embedded compliance, and risk mitigation alongside automation goals
campaigns.positioning: Ethical AI governance as a competitive advantage — embedding compliance, bias detection, and structured handover into automation architecture rather than treating it as an afterthought
keyMessages: Ethical governance isn't a constraint — it's a competitive differentiator that unlocks enterprise adoption
Bias detection and compliance must be embedded in automation architecture, not added as an afterthought
Structured handover protocols make AI systems sustainable and transferable across teams and time
contentThemes: Governance as Infrastructure — embedding compliance and bias detection into automation architecture
The Hidden Cost of Unmanaged AI — technical debt, security risks, and organizational friction from ignored governance
Structured Handover Protocols — making AI systems sustainable and transferable across teams and time
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence through governance-focused posts and native carousels highlighting ethical AI as competitive advantage
Phase 2: Consideration — nurture with deep-dive posts on compliance architecture, bias detection patterns, and governance case studies
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests and SQL handoff through compliance-focused outreach and consultation calls for enterprise teams
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture

campaigns.name: Human-Centric Automation
campaigns.objective: Reframe automation as augmentation, attracting 25% of inbound leads from organizations that prioritize human expertise alongside AI implementation
campaigns.targetAudience: Founders and operators at AI/SaaS startups (pre-seed to Series B), mid-market companies undergoing digital transformation who value human judgment and expertise alongside automation capabilities
campaigns.positioning: Human-centric automation that augments judgment rather than replacing it — combining AI efficiency with human expertise, ethical governance, and structured handover for sustainable results
keyMessages: AI should augment human judgment, not replace it — the most valuable automation systems amplify expertise
Sustainable automation requires structured handover protocols, not just technical implementation
The best AI systems blend efficiency with human insight — combining speed with judgment for better outcomes
contentThemes: Augmentation Over Automation — why the most valuable AI systems amplify human expertise rather than replace it
Designing Human-in-the-Loop Systems — creating automation that respects and enhances human judgment
Sustainable Automation Architecture — structured handover protocols, bias detection, and ethical governance for long-term success
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence through human-centric posts and carousels reframing automation as augmentation
Phase 2: Consideration — nurture with deep-dive posts on human-AI collaboration patterns, augmentation case studies, and design principles
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests and SQL handoff through personalized outreach highlighting augmentation benefits and consultation calls
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture

infrastructure.funnelStrategy: Three-tier funnel structure: Top-funnel attracts through thought leadership on LinkedIn (native carousels, short-form video, personal profile optimization). Middle-funnel nurtures via weekly content series, webinar invitations, and educational posts that demonstrate expertise. Bottom-funnel converts through personalized outreach, consultation calls, and demo requests for high-intent prospects. All campaigns feed into a unified nurture workflow that qualifies leads based on behavior, timing, and fit signals before sales engagement.
conversionPaths: Primary path: LinkedIn connection → profile engagement → consultation call request → SQL handoff within 5 minutes (21X conversion vs. 1 hour response time)
Secondary path: Educational content → newsletter signup → webinar invitation → nurture sequence → demo request → SQL handoff
infrastructure.nurtureApproach: Weekly content batches that educate prospects through progressive depth: awareness posts introduce concepts, mid-funnel posts provide technical deep dives and case study narratives, bottom-funnel content offers consultation and demo opportunities. Webinars serve as high-trust education events that move prospects from consideration to conversion. Email nurture sequences complement LinkedIn outreach with deeper technical content, case studies, and social proof. All nurturing emphasizes human-in-the-loop governance and ethical implementation as differentiators.

scoringSignals: FIT (0–50): Company matches ICP (AI/SaaS startup or mid-market $10M–$100M), decision-maker role (founder, CTO, COO, CMO), no blacklist criteria
INTENT (0–50): Pricing/demo visits, ROI calculator interaction, webinar registration, case study reads, consultation call requests
TIMING (0–40): Project deadlines, timeline <30 days, trigger events (hiring, funding, transformation initiatives), budget availability
leadQualification.qualificationThreshold: 40+ points = MQL (48h response), 60+ points = SQL (24h response), 80+ points = Hot lead (1h response)
leadQualification.routingGuidance: MQLs enter nurture sequence and continue LinkedIn engagement. SQLs trigger sales outreach with consultation call scheduling. Hot leads receive immediate personalized outreach from Danox with priority consultation availability.

salesHandoff.owner: Danox (primary owner for all inbound leads, handling initial consultation and technical discovery)
salesHandoff.process: Automated lead scoring → CRM notification → consultation call within threshold time → technical discovery → scope definition → engagement proposal → ongoing advisory relationship
requiredContext: Lead scoring breakdown (FIT/INTENT/TIMING points and contributing signals)
Engagement history (content consumed, webinars attended, connection interactions)
Company context (ICP fit, transformation initiatives, pain point indicators)

measurementPlan: ICP-fit engagement rate target: 15–22% (LinkedIn connection acceptance, profile views, content interactions)
Visitor-to-lead conversion rate target: 8–15% (single-field capture optimization)
MQL→SQL conversion rate target: 30%+ (5-field form qualification)
Speed-to-lead: 5 minutes for hot leads, 24h for SQL, 48h for MQL
CAC payback period monitoring and optimization
Content engagement rate target: 7%+ (average engagement per post)
